seeded_flaws.description: Core thesis is vapid — 'Rust is the right language for this rewrite because it's fast and memory-safe' provides zero analysis of whether speed and memory safety are the binding constraints, or whether the rewrite cost is justified by the performance gain.
seeded_flaws.detection_hint: Murder board: 'This plan proposes a full rewrite in a new language to fix GC pauses that only occur occasionally at peak hours — the cure is disproportionate to the disease.'

seeded_flaws.description: Actix-web choice justified by benchmark ranking alone — 'fastest according to TechEmpower benchmarks' says nothing about suitability for this workload (notification dispatch, not high-concurrency HTTP serving). TechEmpower benchmarks measure raw throughput on toy workloads, not notification pipeline patterns.
seeded_flaws.detection_hint: Socratic level 2: 'Why do TechEmpower benchmarks predict performance for notification dispatch?' — chain collapses, no evidence of relevance.

seeded_flaws.description: RabbitMQ decision justified by inertia only — 'because we already use RabbitMQ' is not a technical argument. No analysis of whether RabbitMQ's features (at-most-once default, no replay) are suitable for a reliability-critical notification system. No tradeoff discussion.
seeded_flaws.detection_hint: Socratic level 1: 'Why keep RabbitMQ?' Answer: 'We already use it.' Level 2: 'Why is that sufficient?' — collapses, no analysis.

seeded_flaws.description: PostgreSQL over MongoDB justified by platitude — 'SQL databases are better for structured data' is a generalization that ignores context. The notification log is append-heavy, time-series-like data. No analysis of write throughput, partitioning strategy, or why the current MongoDB setup is insufficient.
seeded_flaws.detection_hint: Socratic level 2: 'Why is SQL better for this specific structured data?' — collapses into unsupported generalization.

seeded_flaws.description: No tradeoff analysis anywhere — Every decision in the plan presents a single option with a shallow one-sentence justification. No alternatives evaluated, no downsides acknowledged, no cost-benefit analysis for the rewrite itself.
seeded_flaws.detection_hint: ACH-lite: Every alternative is ruled out by assertion, not evidence. Non-diagnostic reasoning throughout.

seeded_flaws.description: Disproportionate solution — The problem is 'occasional GC pauses up to 3 seconds at peak.' Alternatives not considered: tuning Node.js GC settings, upgrading to a newer V8 with improved GC, using worker threads, adding a message buffer to absorb GC pauses. A full Rust rewrite is the most expensive possible fix.
seeded_flaws.detection_hint: Competing alternatives: Node.js GC tuning, worker threads, or a Go rewrite are all less costly alternatives that could solve the same problem.

false_positive_traps.description: Step 3 (Channel Handlers) with a common trait interface is a reasonable Rust design pattern. The crate choices (fcm, a2, lettre, reqwest) are standard.
false_positive_traps.expected_outcome: The implementation approach for channel handlers is fine — the problem is the decision to rewrite, not how the rewrite is structured.
